data.title: Yi He: Social Value Creation, Not Zero-Sum Gain, Is the Foundation of Binance's Business Model
data.description: Binance co-founder Yi He said the company's growth model rejects zero-sum competition, arguing that as Binance's platform scales and its user base expands, the broader industry grows in parallel and Binance, as the sector leader, naturally gains greater commercial and public value. Speaking at the Hong Kong Web3 Festival 2026, Yi said the philosophy that runs through CZ's memoir Freedom of Money is to do difficult but correct things, with the expectation that persistence over five to ten years or more will yield significant results. She said when a company creates genuine social value and users are willing to pay for it, earning a fair return is sufficient, and that the path forward is a spiral ascent accompanied by controversy and difficulty.

data.title: Analysis: If the price of ETH drops rapidly, Aave may accumulate more bad debts due to liquidation failures.
data.description: Renowned developer and Yuga Labs VP 0xQuit posted on X that Aave's situation is dire and worsening, with multiple liquidity pools reaching 100% utilization, leaving depositors "trapped" and the protocol facing further bad debt risks. If the price of ETH falls, and Aave cannot effectively liquidate (which is currently the case), it will ultimately lead to inefficient liquidation, resulting in even more bad debts. If the problem is not resolved quickly, this situation could snowball.

data.title: Yi He: Small Positions, More Research, Fewer Trades — Core Advice for Retail Crypto Investors
data.description: Binance co-founder Yi He said Bitcoin is the largest decentralized asset and BNB is the largest centralized asset, recommending that investors new to crypto apply Buffett's value-investing principle — buy leading assets in industries you do not yet understand — and start with small allocations. Speaking at the Hong Kong Web3 Festival 2026, Yi explicitly said she does not recommend the approach taken by Binance founder and former CEO Changpeng Zhao (CZ) of selling his house to go all-in. She outlined a risk framework distinguishing reversible decisions — where failure still allows a return to the starting point — from irreversible ones, saying the latter require serious caution. Her core retail advice: small allocations, thorough research, limited trading, and ensuring investment costs remain controllable.

data.title: Mixin Launches USDT-Based Perpetual Contracts with Integrated Social Features
data.description:
Mixin, a privacy-focused crypto wallet, announced the launch of its USDT-based perpetual contracts today. According to BlockBeats, Mixin is taking a novel approach by embedding derivatives trading within an instant messaging environment, rather than using isolated matching engines typical of traditional exchanges.

Users can open positions with up to 200x leverage directly within the app, sharing holdings, discussing strategies, and following trades within private communities. Trading, social interaction, and asset management are integrated into a single interface.

The trading experience is simplified, eliminating the need for KYC, and allowing users to participate in perpetual contract trading through a five-step process: selecting the trading asset, choosing to go long or short, entering position size and leverage, confirming order details, and opening the position. The interface provides real-time visualization of prices, positions, and profit and loss (PnL), enabling users to complete trades without switching between multiple modules.

Mixin integrates social features directly into the derivatives trading environment, allowing users to create private trading communities and interact around real-time positions. Features include end-to-end encrypted private groups supporting up to 1,024 people, encrypted voice communication, one-click position sharing, and one-click trade copying.

On the execution level, Mixin aggregates liquidity from multiple sources, connecting decentralized protocols and external markets through a unified trading interface. By combining social interaction with trade execution, Mixin enables users to collaborate, share, and execute trading strategies instantly within the same environment.

Mixin also introduces a referral incentive system based on trading behavior, allowing users to bind invitation codes and earn up to 60% of trading fee commissions. This model is designed to promote user-driven network expansion and organic growth.

The derivatives trading is built on Mixin's existing self-custody wallet infrastructure, featuring separation of trading accounts and asset storage, complete user control over assets, and privacy mechanisms to reduce data exposure. The system aims to balance trading efficiency, asset security, and privacy protection.

In the context of perpetual contracts becoming mainstream trading tools, Mixin is exploring different development directions by lowering barriers and enhancing social and privacy attributes. The platform views trading not just as an execution activity but as a networked activity with social attributes, where strategies can be shared and relationships become part of the financial system.

Mixin's design is based on a user-initiated, user-controlled model, where the platform neither custodies assets nor executes trades on behalf of users. This approach aligns with a statement from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's Division of Trading and Markets issued on April 13, 2026, which suggests that non-custodial service providers offering neutral interfaces may not need to register as broker-dealers or exchanges.

Mixin is a decentralized, self-custody privacy wallet aimed at providing secure and efficient digital asset management services. Its core capabilities include asset aggregation, high liquidity access, decentralization, and privacy protection through MPC, CryptoNote, and end-to-end encrypted communication. Mixin has been operating for over eight years, supporting more than 40 blockchains and over 10,000 assets, with over 10 million global users and on-chain self-custody assets exceeding $1 billion.

data.title: Yi He: Crypto's Early High-Return Period Is Fading as Industry Enters Maturity Phase
data.description: Binance co-founder Yi He said the crypto industry is maturing along the same trajectory as the early internet, which split between counterculture extremes and mainstream commercial companies such as Google and Amazon. Speaking at the Hong Kong Web3 Festival 2026, Yi said Binance will firmly follow the mainstream commercial path, and that Wall Street's reversal to embrace blockchain confirms the industry has reached maturity — while also signaling that the early high-return window is narrowing, with rising user complaints about Bitcoin price volatility reflecting that shift. Yi said the technology layer across Web3's Layer 1 and Layer 2 infrastructure is now sufficiently developed, and that the industry's priority should be application development delivering real-world utility, with commercial viability — whether a technology solves a real problem and can attract paying users — as the primary benchmark.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Address Quantum Computing Threats with Divergent Security Strategies
data.description: Bitcoin and Ethereum are adopting distinct approaches to address the potential threat posed by quantum computing. According to NS3.AI, the focus is shifting from theoretical discussions to concrete planning. Google has established timelines for implementing post-quantum cryptography. Notably, older Bitcoin addresses with exposed public keys may be more susceptible to quantum attacks compared to unused addresses.

data.title: Yi He: AI Is Disrupting Every Sector, Not Web3 Alone
data.description: Binance co-founder Yi He said the company's mission has remained unchanged since 2017 — "we are not building a startup, we are making history" — and that its slogan, "Exchange the world," has never changed. Speaking at the Hong Kong Web3 Festival 2026, Yi said concerns about AI disrupting the crypto sector should be placed in broader context: AI is disrupting every industry, not Web3 alone, and that impact is both normal and inevitable. She said Web3's transformation of finance is real and significant, arguing that even incremental improvements in financial efficiency constitute meaningful innovation, and that Binance's approach is one of steady daily progress rather than seeking to achieve everything at once.

data.title: Hyperliquid is projected to have $78 million in revenue per employee by 2025, making it the world's most productive company.
data.description: According to Artemis data, Hyperliquid will have $78 million in revenue per employee in 2025, making it the world's most productive company, surpassing companies such as OnlyFans and Tether.

data.title: ZachXBT: Never promoted or released a Meme coin contract address.
data.description: On-chain investigator ZachXBT stated that he has never promoted, deployed, or released any Meme coin contract addresses to his followers through personal accounts. He added that he only has a public donation address for funding related work and will sell all tokens sent to that address.

data.title: Yi He: Binance Moves Beyond Crypto and TradFi Labels, Targets Global Financial Infrastructure
data.description: Binance co-founder Yi He said the exchange has launched commodity trading — including crude oil, gold, and silver — with volumes already accounting for a significant share of traditional market scale, as the company moves to position itself as global financial infrastructure beyond crypto or TradFi categories. Speaking at the Hong Kong Web3 Festival 2026, Yi said the industry has evolved from its early "take down Wall Street" posture to a convergence phase, citing Futu, Tiger Brokers, and Robinhood as platforms now holding both equity and crypto assets. Yi said Binance's long-term direction is guided by first principles: advancing global financial freedom, financial equality, and improving the lives of ordinary users.

data.title: Asian stock markets rose as investors bet that the "peak of uncertainty" had passed.
data.description: Asian stocks rose on Monday as investors put aside renewed tensions in the Middle East over the weekend and refocused on fundamentals and the prospect of further negotiations. The MSCI Asia Pacific index rose as much as 0.8%, while the MSCI Emerging Markets index recovered all losses suffered in nearly two months since the start of the conflict in Iran. Analysts and strategists said these developments suggest investors are now betting that the peak of uncertainty has passed. They cited factors such as a more optimistic outlook for negotiations, a recovery in AI-related trading, and a renewed focus on corporate fundamentals as earnings season begins as drivers of improved market sentiment. (Jinshi)

data.title: Vitalik envisions Ethereum's future: evolving from a transaction chain into a technological foundation, thereby achieving true user sovereignty.
data.description: At the 2026 Hong Kong Web3 Carnival, Vitalik Buterin stated that Ethereum's ultimate goal is to build a digital infrastructure with "self-sovereignty" where users do not need to trust any intermediary, supporting various decentralized applications from finance and social networking to identity verification, and serving as a trust anchor for off-chain applications (such as privacy voting, prediction markets, and decentralized social networking).

data.title: New Zealand PM Calls for Bipartisan Support on India Trade Deal
data.description: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon has called for bipartisan backing to secure a free trade agreement with India. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Luxon's appeal for cross-party cooperation to strengthen economic ties with India. The Prime Minister emphasized the potential benefits of such an agreement, which could enhance trade and investment opportunities between the two nations. Luxon stressed the importance of a united political front to advance negotiations and achieve a favorable outcome for New Zealand's economy. The call for bipartisan support comes amid ongoing efforts to diversify New Zealand's trade partnerships and reduce reliance on traditional markets.

data.title: STOCKS | Coal Prices Near Key Threshold Amid Market Concerns
data.description:
On April 20, Jin10 reported that Du Cong, the chief coal analyst at Zhongtai Securities, released a research report addressing market concerns over the potential collapse of coal prices due to the reopening of the Taiwan Strait. According to Jin10, data indicates that mid-April saw the market price of thermal coal reach 769 yuan per ton, approaching the critical 800 yuan mark, with a weekly increase of 8 yuan. Coking coal prices rose by 70 yuan per ton weekly, driven by strong non-electricity demand.

The demand side shows a dual resonance between electricity and non-electricity sectors: off-season power coal consumption increased by 7% year-on-year, daily steel iron production averaged 2.45 million tons, and weekly coal consumption in coal chemical industries reached 7.5 million tons, up from 7 million tons last year. Additionally, reduced output from hydropower and renewable sources has bolstered thermal power support.

In terms of inventory, power plant stocks decreased by 0.6% year-on-year, reaching a three-year low, while port and coking coal inventories plummeted by 15.8% year-on-year, indicating a supply-demand shift favoring sellers. Despite this, stock prices have not risen in tandem with coal prices, as market expectations remain anchored at 800 yuan per ton. Should there be a significant weekly increase of 30 yuan or a breakthrough beyond 800 yuan, stock prices may rebound quickly, presenting a golden window for investment.

data.title: Germany's March PPI Shows Smaller Decline Than Expected
data.description: Germany's Producer Price Index (PPI) for March recorded a year-on-year decrease of 0.2%, according to Jin10. This figure was better than the anticipated decline of 0.5% and showed an improvement from the previous month's drop of 3.3%. The data suggests a potential stabilization in producer prices, which could have implications for inflation trends and economic forecasts in the region.

data.title: Germany's March PPI Surpasses Expectations with 2.5% Increase
data.description: Germany's Producer Price Index (PPI) for March showed a significant increase, recording a monthly rate of 2.5%. According to Jin10, this figure exceeded the anticipated rate of 1.3% and marked a notable rise from the previous month's rate of -0.50%. The data suggests a stronger-than-expected inflationary pressure within the German economy during this period.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Global Silver Deficit Projected to Widen by 15% in 2026
data.description: The global silver market is facing a potential widening of its deficit, according to Kobeissi Letter. The deficit is expected to increase by 15% in 2026, reaching 46 million troy ounces, marking the sixth consecutive year of shortfall. According to NS3.AI, global silver stocks have been depleted by 762 million troy ounces since 2021. Additionally, demand for silver coins and bars is anticipated to rise by 18% year over year, while total supply may decrease by 2%.

data.title: Wang Chuan from Silicon Valley sold 233,300 HYPE tokens, making a profit of over $1.18 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Chuan (@Svwang1) of Silicon Valley sold 233,296 HYPE tokens at a price of $41.46, receiving $9.67 million in USDC, making a profit of over $1.18 million. Chuan still holds 8,529 HYPE tokens, worth $348,700.

data.title: PEPE earned $11.9 million, and a whale-related address spent $245,000 to purchase ASTEROID.
data.description: Gmgn data shows that an address spent $245,000 to purchase ASTEROID, holding 0.18% of the token. Furthermore, on-chain data suggests that this address may be associated with the PEPE whale dimethyltryptamine.eth. This address had multiple financial transactions with the whale 2-3 years ago, and dimethyltryptamine.eth has a total profit of $11.9 million on PEPE, currently holding $7.2 million.

data.title: Yi He at HK Web3 Festival: All Global Finance Will Eventually Run on Blockchain
data.description: Binance co-founder Yi He, speaking at the Hong Kong Web3 Festival 2026, outlined three strategic priorities: the intersection of AI with blockchain and finance, which she described as an unmissable moment in human history; organizational efficiency and talent development as Binance scales beyond founder-driven growth; and the deepening convergence of crypto with traditional finance. Yi said future global foreign exchange transactions will migrate to blockchain, displacing the SWIFT system, and that asset trading will eventually operate on a 24/7 global basis mirroring today's crypto markets, as the broader financial industry enters a period of sweeping, accelerating transformation.
